title: common: add support for `default_host_ips` in containers.conf
body:
This adds support for configuring a default host IP via containers.conf to bind published container ports to when no host IP is explicitly specified (e.g. -p 8000:8000). Note that explicit host IP still overrides the default option set in containers.conf.

Refers https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27186

<!--- Please read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) before proceeding --->

Do you have podman PR ready that uses this field? We merged in the past nmew fields without a ready to go podman PR causing the docs to be released without actually being integrated into podman which causes confusion for users of course. So I like to wait with the final merge until we know podman will be ready as well.

Fair point, I had the commit ready in my fork back when this was new. I've rebased, updated, and raised the PR for a review - https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27938.

How would the merge process work? Because the podman PR will continue to fail until this one is merged.

---

Additionally, the [dual-stack PR](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/422#discussion_r2486266157) that was also briefly mentioned here is currently blocked waiting for a response. It'd be helpful to move that ahead, considering we initially planned to get it ready for 6.0.
